problem.title: Number of Islands II
problem.description: You are given an empty 2D binary grid of size m x n. The grid represents a map where 0's represent water and 1's represent land. Land cells are added one at a time via positions; after each addition return the number of islands.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Union Find
solutions.description: Maintain a Union-Find structure over m×n cells. When a new land cell is added, increment the island count and then try to union it with each adjacent land neighbor — each successful union (different components) decrements the count. Path compression and union by rank keep each operation near O(1) amortized (O(α(m×n))). The key insight is that Union-Find naturally handles dynamic connectivity as land is added incrementally.
